Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 5, 244 [unidentified incantation or ritual][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[...] ⸢tala₂⸣-pap

(o 1) You wrap ...

o 22

[...] ⸢u₂?⸣[...]

(o 2) (1 line fragmentary)

o 33

[...] x ⸢na₄?⸣[... NA₄.d]⸢LAMMA?⸣ na₄ZU₂ BABBAR

(o 3) ..., protective-deity-stone, white flint,

o 44

[...] x na₄IGI.KU₆

(o 4) ... and eye-of-a-fish-stone.

o 55

[... ma]-ši-tu₂ ḫa-⸢sa-su⸣ ar₂-ra-⸢at⸣ [x]

(o 5) ..., for remembering what has been forgotten, for ... a curse,

o 66

[...] x DU₈ar₂ NA₄ ⸢ŠU⸣.SI ḪULtu₂ ana EGIR la₃!(PAB) [x]

(o 6) and for releasing ..., (use) the stone not ... an evil finger behind the back.

o 77

[...]-tu₂ LU₂ la₃ DU₁₁.GA NA₄-MEŠ LU₂ ina ḪUL₂ ŠA₃ x [x]

(o 7) For not saying ... to a person, (use) the stones ... a person in a contented heart.

o 88

[...] i-zi-im-ta-šu₂ KURad₂ LU₂!-MEŠ KI DINGIR SILIM1

(o 8) ..., for fulfilling his desire, for reconciling people to a god,

o 99

[...] sul-li₃-im-ma un-ni-ni-šu₂ ŠE.GA niš ⸢ŠU⸣-MIN-šu₂ ma-ḫa-ri

(o 9) for praying ..., as well as for hearing his petitions, for receiving the raising of his hands (in supplication),

o 1010

[...] ra-a-mi iš-⸢di-iḫ⸣-šu₂ ana šul-lu-⸢mu⸣

(o 10) for loving ..., for making his business flourish,

o 1111

[U₃.MA]-⸢šu₂⸣ ana KURad₂ NA₄ ⸢ḫat?-tu₂?⸣ x pi?-rit-<tu₂> ⸢la₃ TE⸣ kip-⸢di⸣ EN DU₁₁-[šu₂ šu-ud]-⸢de⸣-e

(o 11) and for achieving his triumph, (use) the stone for not letting panic or terror near and making his adversary abandon (his) schemes.

o 1212

[...]-⸢i⸣ NA₄ ḪUL ŠA₃ ana LU₂ la₃ TEe NA₄-MEŠ NIR.GAL₂ ana!(ŠU₂) DU.DU-ma

(o 12) For ..., (use) the stone for a troubled heart not to affect a person and the stones to proceed in eminence as well as

o 1313

[...] IGI-šu₂ ḫa-de-e NA₄? x x x MA x x x x kal u₄-mu lu!-u₂ kal mu-ši

(o 13) ... to welcome seeing him ... all day or all night.

o 1414

[...] ⸢TE⸣e NA₄-ME ḪUL.GIG DI.BAL.A ZI.KU₅.RU.DA.A u KA.DAB.BE₂.DA

(o 14) For letting ... near, (use) the stones for (not) letting hate, injustice, throat-cutting and mouth-blocking near, and also for not letting any evil deeds fix on his hands.

o 1515

[... TE]e u mim-ma ep-šet ḪULtu₂ ina ŠU-MIN-šu₂ la₃ GAL₂

o 1616

[...] ina maḫ-ri UN-MEŠ-šu₂ IGI.TAB-ma UGU UN-MEŠ-šu₂ a-qa-ri-šu₂

(o 16) For overseeing ... in the presence of his countrymen, as well as for being honoured over his countrymen,

o 1717

[...] e-ma DUku ŠE.GA NA₄-MEŠ <šu>-ṭu!-<ub>-bi at-me-e EN DU₁₁-šu₂

(o 17) and ... for being agreeable wherever he goes, (use) the stones for sweetening the words of his adversary.

o 1818

[x?]+5 NA₄-MEŠ ŠEŠ-MEŠ ina DUR GADA DUR sig₂ḪE₂.ME.DA DUR sig₂ZA.GIN₃.NA

(o 18) (You thread) these ... (previously mentioned) stone-beads on a string of linen, a string of red-purple wool and a string of blue-purple wool.

o 1919

[...] ⸢u₂SIKIL⸣ GIŠ.BUR₂ U₂.dUTU

(o 19) You wrap ..., pure-plant, wood(-for)-releasing, Sun-plant and tarmuš-plant, these 5 materials, and ... and red-purple wool, between each (bead). You put them at each side of the bed.

o 2020

[... u₂tar]-muš 5 U₂-MEŠ ŠEŠ-MEŠ

o 2121

[...] ⸢sig₂⸣ḪE₂.ME.DA ina bi-ri-šu₂-nu tala₂-pap A₂ ana A₂ ša₂? ⸢NU₂?⸣ GUB

o 2222

[... bi]-ri?-šu₂-nu tu-kaṣ₃-ṣar! u₂KUR.KUR u₂LU₂nu u₂IGI-lim

(o 22) You tie ... between each. You put atā'išu-plant, man-like-plant, it-withstands-a-thousand(-illnesses)-plant, ... and it-withstands-20(-illnesses)-plant, these 5 plants, in oil-from-a-bowl and fine oil.

o 2323

[... u₂]⸢IGI⸣.20 5 U₂-ME ŠEŠ ana ŠA₃ I₃ BUR u I₃ DU₁₀.GA ŠUBdi-ma

o 2424

[...] ⸢EN₂⸣-MEŠ an-na-a-tu₂ 3-TA.AM₃ ana muḫ-ḫi NA₄-MEŠ ŠIDnu

(o 24) .... You recite these incantations 3 times over the beads.

o 2525

[...] ⸢MI?⸣ E₂-šu₂ GARan NA₄-MEŠ tul₂-lal₂ ina IGI ddele-bat

(o 25) You put ... of his house. You purify the beads before Venus.

o 2626

[...] ⸢šim⸣LI GARan KAŠ SAG BALqi₂ EN₂ DINGIR-MU ŠE.GA

(o 26) You provide ... juniper. You pour out best beer. (You recite) the incantation 'My god is willing'.

o 2727

[...] x-šu₂? x KA KA MA A ZU NE

(o 27) ... his ...

o 2828

[...] x AN? GIR? EN NA MIN

(o 28) (1 line fragmentary)

o 2929

[... 3]-⸢TA⸣.AM₃ ana UGU NA₄-MEŠ ŠIDnu

(o 29) ... You recite ... 3 times over the beads.

o 3030

[...] ⸢e⸣-ma DUku ma-gir

(o 30) ... wherever he goes he will be agreeable.

Reverse
r 1r 1

[...] x-MEŠ i-ta-mu-⸢u₂?⸣

(r 1) They discuss ....

r 22

[...] ḪUL la₃ TE⸢e⸣

(r 2) ... evil not to approach.

r 33

[...] ⸢DINGIR? a?⸣-bi-šu₂ ŠE.⸢GA?⸣

(r 3) ... the god of his father is willing.

r 44

[...] x

(r 4) (5 lines fragmentary)

r 55

[...] x x

r 66

[...] ⸢ḪI?⸣

r 77

[...] ⸢ŠA₃?⸣

r 88

[...] TE I? x AB

r 99

[...] GA x

(r 9) (2 lines fragmentary)

r 1010

[...] ⸢E?⸣

r 1111

[...] BI? x

r 1313

[...]

r 1414

[...]

r 1515

[...] li-nu-ḫu

(r 15) May they rest ....

r 1616

[...] NA

(r 16) (3 lines fragmentary)

r 1717

[...] x x

r 1818

[...] TE x

r 1919

[...] x NA₄ BABBAR

(r 19) ... white stone
